results.question: Which countries are subject to anti-dumping investigation on electrogalvanized steel imports into India?
results.expected_src: Anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of Electrogalvanized Steel from Korea RP, Japan and Singapore
results.expected_ans: Korea RP, Japan, and Singapore are subject to the anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of electrogalvanized steel into India.
results.actual_answer:
According to Source 1, Notice for Oral Hearing_DVC_WEBEX_Electrogalvanized Steel_12.04.2022.pdf, the countries subject to anti-dumping investigation on electrogalvanized steel imports into India are Korea RP, Japan, and Singapore (Source 1, page 0, "Subject: Anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of Electrogalvanized Steel originating in or exported from Korea RP, Japan, and Singapore"). 

This information is also confirmed in Source 2, List of registered interested parties and sharing of NCV_EGS_29.03.2022.pdf, (page 0, "Subject: Anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of Electrogalvanized Steel from Korea RP, Japan, and Singapore.") and Source 3, NON-CONFIDENTIAL_FINAL FINDINGS_EG STEEL_27.07.2022.pdf, (page 0, "Subject: Anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of “Electrogalvanized Steel” from Korea RP, Japan and Singapore.").
sources: List of registered interested parties and sharing of NCV_EGS_29.03.2022.pdf

results.question: What products are covered under the anti-dumping investigation on seamless tubes from China?
results.expected_src: Anti Dumping investigation on imports of Seamless Tubes etc. of Iron, Alloy or Non-Alloy Steel originating in or exported from China PR
results.expected_ans: The investigation covers seamless tubes, pipes, and hollow profiles of iron, alloy or non-alloy steel originating in or exported from China PR.
results.actual_answer:
According to [Source 3] seamless tube pipes NCV.pdf (page 0), the products covered under the anti-dumping investigation are Seamless Tubes, pipes & Hollow profiles of Iron, Alloy or Non-Alloy Steel (other than cast iron and stainless steel), whether hot finished or cold drawn or cold rolled, with an external diameter not exceeding 355.6 mm or 14" OD, originating in or exported from China PR. 

Additionally, [Source 1] FF-NCV.pdf (page 16) notes that high pressure gas cylinder seamless tubes are excluded from the scope of the product under consideration. Also, secondary pipes, defective, rusted and used pipes are part of the product under consideration and cannot be excluded while examining the import statistics.

results.question: What type of products are covered by the safeguard investigation on steel flat products?
results.expected_src: Initiation of safeguard investigation concerning imports of Non-Alloy and Alloy Steel Flat Products into India
results.expected_ans: The safeguard investigation covers imports of Non-Alloy and Alloy Steel Flat Products into India.
results.actual_answer:
According to [Source 1] Preliminary Findings_1.pdf (page 7), the products under consideration in the safeguard investigation are "Non-Alloy and Alloy Steel Flat Products" comprising of five product categories, namely: 
(a) Hot Rolled (“HR”) coils, sheets and plates, 
(b) HR Plate Mill Plates, 
(c) Cold Rolled (“CR”) coils and sheets, 
(d) Metallic Coated Steel coils and sheets, 
(e) Colour Coated coils and sheets, whether or not profiled, including Galvanneal, Coated with Zinc or Aluminium-Zinc or Zinc-Aluminium-Magnesium.

results.question: What does PCN stand for and why is it used in anti-dumping investigations?
results.expected_src: Anti-dumping investigation concerning imports of Electrogalvanized Steel from Korea RP, Japan and Singapore
results.expected_ans: PCN stands for Product Control Number (or Product Classification Number). It is used in anti-dumping investigations to systematically classify different types/grades of the subject product so that like-for-like price comparisons can be made between the imported product and the domestically produced product.

results.question: What is the difference between an anti-dumping measure and a safeguard measure?
results.expected_ans: An anti-dumping measure targets imports from specific countries found to be dumped (sold below normal value) causing material injury to domestic industry. A safeguard measure is a temporary restriction applied to imports from all countries when a sudden surge in imports causes or threatens serious injury to domestic industry, regardless of whether dumping is occurring.
results.actual_answer:
According to FF-NCV.pdf (page 16), the difference between an anti-dumping measure and a safeguard measure lies in their purpose and scope. Both aim to provide a level playing field to the domestic industry, but they differ in their application. 

Safeguard duty is imposed on goods that enter in increased quantities and cause or threaten to cause serious injury to the domestic industry producing like or directly competitive goods. 

On the other hand, anti-dumping duty is imposed on dumped imported goods, where dumping occurs when goods are exported by a country to another country at a price lower than its normal value.

Additionally, as noted in Preliminary Findings_1.pdf (page 11) and NCV FINAL SGD Steel 16.08.2025.pdf (page 46), the analysis of "likeness" under the Anti-Dumping Agreement focuses on product characteristics, whereas the Agreement on Safeguards considers the competitive relationship between products.
results.context:
[Source 1] FF-NCV.pdf (page 16)

the Authority notes that the purpose and scope of anti -dumping and safeguard 
investigations are broadly the same i.e. to provide level playing field to the 
domestic industry vis-à-vis imported goods. Safeguard duty is imposed on goods 
which enter in increased quantities and cause or threaten to cause serious injury 
to domestic industry producing like or directly competitive goods. Antidumping 
duty is imposed on the dumped i mported goods and dumping is said to occur 
when the goods are exported by a country to another country at a price lower 
than its normal value.

results.question: What is the IS 2062 standard and which steel products does it apply to?
results.expected_ans: IS 2062 is the Bureau of Indian Standards specification for hot rolled medium and high tensile structural steel. It applies to structural steel products used in fabrication of bridges, transmission line towers, railway wagons, industrial structures, and similar applications.

results.question: What triggers the initiation of a safeguard investigation in India?
results.expected_src: Initiation of safeguard investigation concerning imports of Non-Alloy and Alloy Steel Flat Products into India
results.expected_ans: A safeguard investigation in India is initiated when there is sufficient evidence of a surge in imports of a product causing or threatening serious injury to the domestic industry. A petition is filed by the domestic industry, and the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) examines the evidence before deciding to initiate a formal investigation.
results.actual_answer:
According to [Source 1] Preliminary Findings_1.pdf (page 0), the initiation of a safeguard investigation in India is triggered by an allegation of a "recent, sudden, sharp and significant increase in the volume of imports, which has caused serious injury to the domestic industry in India." Additionally, it is alleged that imports have taken place in such increased quantities and under such circumstances as to cause and threaten to cause serious injury to the domestic industry. 

This is further supported by [Source 2] NCV FINAL SGD Steel 16.08.2025.pdf (page 1), which mentions that the Authority initiated the investigation vide initiation notification No. 22/01/2024-DGTR dated 19 December 2024. 

However, the specific trigger is mentioned in [Source 1] as the allegation of serious injury to the domestic industry caused by the increased imports.

results.question: What organisation conducts anti-dumping and safeguard investigations in India?
results.expected_ans: The Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR), under the Ministry of Commerce and Industry, conducts anti-dumping and safeguard investigations in India.
results.actual_answer: According to Source 1 (Trade Notice 08_2021_User Industry.pdf, page 1), the organisation that conducts anti-dumping investigations in India is the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR). Additionally, Source 2 (FF-NCV.pdf, page 33) mentions that the Director General of Safeguards conducts safeguard investigations. However, it can be inferred that the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR) is the overarching organisation, as the Director General of Safeguards is not mentioned as a separate entity in Source 1, and the DGTR's website and contact information are provided. Therefore, the answer is the Directorate General of Trade Remedies (DGTR).

results.question: How is normal value determined in India's anti-dumping investigations?
results.expected_ans: Normal value in India's anti-dumping investigations is typically the price at which the product is sold in the domestic market of the exporting country. If domestic sales are insufficient or below cost, normal value may be constructed based on cost of production plus reasonable amounts for selling, general and administrative expenses and profit.

results.question: What is the injury margin concept in anti-dumping investigations?
results.expected_ans: The injury margin in anti-dumping investigations is the difference between the non-injurious price (NIP) of the domestic industry and the landed value of imports. It represents the price at which imports should have been priced to not cause injury to domestic industry. The lesser duty rule may apply, where the anti-dumping duty is limited to the injury margin if it is lower than the dumping margin.
results.actual_answer:
The concept of injury margin in anti-dumping investigations is mentioned in Source 2: FF_CR_NCV.pdf (page 10), which states "determination of dumping margin and injury margin." However, the provided context does not explicitly define or explain the injury margin concept. 

Source 3: Initiation Notification_Electrogalvanized Steel.pdf (page 3) mentions that the purpose of an anti-dumping investigation is to "recommend the amount of antidumping duty, which if levied, would be adequate to remove the injury to the domestic industry." This implies that the injury margin is related to the amount of duty required to counteract the injury caused by dumping, but a clear definition is not provided.

Therefore, based on the given context, the injury margin concept is only mentioned in Source 2, but its definition or explanation is not provided.

results.question: What raw materials does India's steel industry primarily depend on?
results.expected_ans: India's steel industry primarily depends on iron ore, coking coal, limestone, and scrap steel. India is largely self-sufficient in iron ore but heavily dependent on imports of coking coal, primarily from Australia and the USA.

results.question: How does the EU Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism affect Indian steel exports to Europe?
results.expected_ans: The EU CBAM imposes a carbon price on imported goods from sectors including steel, based on the carbon content of the product. Indian steel exporters to the EU must report embedded carbon emissions during the transitional phase and will eventually pay a carbon price equivalent to the EU ETS price. This increases the cost of Indian steel exports to the EU and incentivises decarbonisation of Indian steel production.

results.question: What are the reporting requirements for Indian steel exporters under EU CBAM?
results.expected_ans: Under EU CBAM, Indian steel exporters must report the quantity of goods exported and the embedded greenhouse gas emissions in those goods. During the transitional period (2023-2025), only reporting is required with no financial obligation. From 2026, importers must purchase CBAM certificates corresponding to the carbon price that would have been paid under EU carbon pricing rules.

results.question: Which HS chapter has a higher average MFN tariff in India — HS 72 (Iron and Steel) or HS 73 (Articles of Iron or Steel)?
results.expected_ans: HS 73 (Articles of Iron or Steel) has a significantly higher average MFN tariff at approximately 10.98% in 2023, compared to HS 72 (Iron and Steel) at approximately 6.80% in 2023.
results.actual_answer: HS chapter 73 (Articles of Iron or Steel) has a higher average MFN tariff of 10.98% compared to HS chapter 72 (Iron and Steel) with an average tariff of 6.80%.
results.judge_reason: The actual answer accurately reflects the information that would be expected, directly comparing the average MFN tariffs of HS 72 and HS 73 in India, with correct values and a logical conclusion.
